descriptions.value:
Your adventure starts in Antaloor's far north, in the mountainous region of Thalmont. A
mysterious letter, which was the first clue of your twin sister Kyra's whereabouts for many
months, has led you to the small village of Komorin.

descriptions.name: Bounty Hunter
descriptions.type: Two Worlds: Epic Edition Trading Card

descriptions.value:
So let me tell you a little about myself. First off my name is Ayumi. I'm the best treasure hunter there is - and a virtuoso with my gunblades. I can shoot with them and fight
my way through the massed ranks of my enemies - I call that my "Slash Dance", by the way... and like I told you before, a girl should do what she does best. Huh? Dangerous? Of course it's dangerous! But can you see me in a boring job? Me? No WAY!

Well, I was soon to find out that I wasn't quite as alone on the island as I'd thought...
Normally, if I'd found competition here... well, let's just say I might have got a little upset... but Jay played an important role in my adventure right away - and he IS kinda cute I suppose. If only he'd stop being so... so MACHO with that light magic of his!

I heard legends that say these warriors were once a primitive but peaceful race. I guess the
Powers of the Dark changed them forever.
Thankfully for me, they're not built for hard
combat and are only dangerous in larger numbers.
OK, the Light wouldn't win too many popularity contests... but The Dark now... that's one NASTY being! And he actually guards the Artifact of Light! Kind of ironic, huh?

I never expected anything like this guy! He looks like a lion, with runes on his skin... and he guards the Artifact of the Dark. He's a Master of Magic - and you better believe he can really cast effective spells!
Geez! The Pangos are extremely nervous, chronically hungry creatures. Don't let them fool you just because they have no magic.

This machine gets me so mad! It really doesn't
create monsters but teleports them to battle a
threat. Mainly ME! Regardless of how it works it's equipped with defensive spells and can only be destroyed by blades.

I hate these machines, because the Dark uses them to control his icky minions. Supposedly these turned the Pangolins into servants of evil. All I know is that they're armed with machine guns but thanks to their stupid
design they have a slow reaction time and their microchips shortcircuiting with electricity.

Eww! It looks like a one-eyed dragonfly, but despite having an eye they seem almost blind.
It's as if they track me by the heat I am emitting or something. The eye is used as a weapon to shoot energy at me. I find shooting back with my guns and spells put them down pretty easy.
... When J. is possessed, you can only defeat him with Light magic – use the Ray of Light at the very least.
Ha! I've noticed that these cloned versions are only half the size of the originals. Perhaps the Dark was reluctant to make copies of the ancient Galapagos due to their destructive tendencies.

Rogdor is the undisputed leader of the remaining Orcs in Antaloor. He's obviously a great warrior; otherwise he could never have advanced through the Orc ranks to his exalted position. Whether he's wielding a two-handed sword, swinging a huge axe or attacking with two one-handed weapons, Rogdor is the embodiment of the perfect fighting machine - and this is one real hard guy to stop!

descriptions.name: Rogdor
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Nortar is an orcish master of Archery

descriptions.name: Nortar
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Kyra is the Hero's twin sister in Two Worlds II. In spite of a self-sacrificing battle against evil, the twins were separated and Kyra is now under the influence of a powerful magic spell from which there is no escape. The whole main storyline revolves around saving her from Gandohar.

descriptions.name: Kyra
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Dragons are rare in Antaloor, but if you meet them be very careful - they are powerful.

descriptions.name: Dragon
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value:
The Myrmidon is a colossus. It is almost 10 feet in height and has enough
weapons to start its own armory.
At first glance you might think that this monster is sluggish and no danger to
you - but it moves surprisingly fast in spite of its size and mass.

descriptions.name: Myrrmidon
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Gandohar is the main antagonist, for most of Two Worlds II. He has placed himself as the Emperor of Antaloor. He has taken on a more evil looking appearance and rules with an iron fist. During his reign he has commited several nefarious deeds such as offically disbanding the Mages Guild and wiping out nearly the entire Orcish race.

descriptions.name: Gandohar (Trading Card)
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value:
Groms are the rats of Antaloor. They’re everywhere and they can be very annoying,
even up to a point where they can even make your adventurer’s life difficult! Their
strength is in numbers and hordes of these creatures lurk in wait for the unwary
traveler.

descriptions.name: Grom Warrior
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Two Worlds II starts with a mass battle in which Rogdors best friend lost his life.

descriptions.name: Mass Battle
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Ghortarius is an orcish master of Magic arts.

descriptions.name: Ghortarius
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value:
The Scythe is yet another demonic spawn of Verita. It has no hands at the end of
its arms, just razor-sharp blades - and these are often the last things an opponent ever
sees!

descriptions.name: Scythe
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Sordahon is the embodiment of pure evil - and the inhabitants of Antaloor fear him like no other. His merciless domination and overwhelming urge to destroy have rightfully earned him the title of the “Dark Lord”. Usually armed with a large mace and a gigantic shield, he leads Gandohar's armies throughout the land, spreading terror wherever he goes.

descriptions.name: Sordahon
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: This Monster you will find Tier Geal.

descriptions.name: Swamp Demon
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Zombies are distributed throughout the entire country. They are usually creatures of the night. However, since the Taint outbreak, they have also been seen in the daytime, mainly in cemeteries, where they lie in wait for their victims, usually accompanied by a horde of Skeletons. Zombies are slow, but they have a huge reach with their long, ape-like arms. And it doesn’t matter what type of creature ends up in their foul-smelling embrace – because their fetid claws are highly poisonous. Zombies can be even more dangerous in the countless systems of caves in Antaloor.

descriptions.name: Zombie
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value:
This mysterious and extremely attractive half-Orc assassin really knows how to surprise her opponents, pouncing from the shadows to deliver her deadly attack. 
 Dar Pha had a very hard childhood - and it still affects her life today. Although we can’t reveal too much about her past life right now, let’s just say that everything that's happened to this bewitching warrior lady in the past would make ideal material for one of the best adventure novels you’ve ever read!

descriptions.name: Darpha
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

descriptions.value: Cassara is the leader of the remaining orcs of Gor Gammar. She is a seeress and can tell the possible outcome of the future, which she shows to the hero. She is also a dragon, and probably one of the last.

descriptions.name: Cassara (Trading Card)
descriptions.type: Two Worlds II HD Trading Card

Where's the canary
Watch out for Big Daddy.
Even clones have ghosts
Where all the clones live.

descriptions.value: Kara is the first human gravitymancer to attempt the gauntlet.
The Gravity Well is the tool of the gravitymancer.  Only those with battle-hardened skills can traverse the gauntlet with only this in their arsenal.
This device is the one most often used by the intermediate gravitymancer to push objects away.  It also allows the user to hover in place while contemplating their next move.
Shields the gravitymancer from the harsh G-forces sustains as a part of the training regimen.

Only the most dexterous and able-bodied of pupils have been able to attain the master rank of gravitymancer and evade Fibonacci's Death Spiral.  

This is one of the first steps the Felinian race has devised to test the true potential of a gravitymancer's ability.
Capital ship of the Trader Emergency Coalition tasked with relaying civilian colonists to new worlds.

descriptions.name: Akkan Battlecruiser
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Quickly blitzing unwary targets, the Vasari's Antorak Marauder is perfectly suited for assisting stronger vessels in deadly strikes.

descriptions.name: Antorak Marauder
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: A recent addition to the TEC armada, the Corsev is a converted freighter which now houses squads of marines. When near a weakened foe, the marines are launched in boarding pods to capture the opposing ship.

descriptions.name: Corsev Battlecruiser
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The newest Advent weapon to enter the war, the Discord features a number of devastating psionic abilities that aid in its destruction of enemy ships.

descriptions.name: Discord Battleship
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Once a cargo freighter, the Dunov now serves the TEC as a support battlecruiser, helping to fortify its accompanying fleet.

descriptions.name: Dunov Battlecruiser
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The largest capital ship known to exist in the galaxy, the Jarrasul once projected the power of the Vasari Empire to each of its worlds. Now it ferries the exodus fleet away from their nemesis.

descriptions.name: Jarrasul Evacuator
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The first dedicated warship design in nearly 700 years, the TEC count on the Kol's balance of speed, protection and firepower to carry it through many battles.

descriptions.name: Kol Battleship
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The lead assault class vessel for the Vasari, the Kortul combines a full complement of heavy weapons with impressive defensive systems to wear down its opponents.

descriptions.name: Kortul Devastator
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Once a small planetoid destruction and recovery workhorse for the Trade Order, the Marza has been retrofitted to bring crushing firepower to hostile worlds.

descriptions.name: Marza Dreadnought
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The Progenitor Mothership taps directly into the Advent Unity's collective consciousness, allowing it to spread the message and will of the Advent to new planets.

descriptions.name: Progenitor Mothership
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The Radiance personifies the Advent's vengeful nature. Literally bristling with energy weapons, it can unleash a torrential downpour of fire onto enemy ships.

descriptions.name: Radiance Battleship
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Relatively new to the Vasari fleet, the Rankulas primary weapon is its unique nano-reactor. This reactor is capable of creating swarms of offensive and defensive nanites.

descriptions.name: Rankulas Battlecruiser
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Lightly armed compared to other Advent warships, the Rapture Battlecruiser's true strength is its advanced PsiTech.

descriptions.name: Rapture Battlecruiser
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Equipped with immense arrays of powerful PsiTech projectors, the Advent's Revelation battlecruiser is capable of inflicting chaos on the population of hapless worlds.

descriptions.name: Revelation Battlecruiser
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: Few ships in the galaxy can match the destructive potential of the Vasari's Vulkoras Desolator. Both enemy ships and planets fall quickly before its banks of heavy phase missiles and deployable siege batteries.

descriptions.name: Vulkoras Desolator
descriptions.type: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion

descriptions.value: The ambush in Blackwood Forest.
A perilous journey through the Jubisaro Caverns.
Discover true evil.
A hidden evil lurks below.
Evil's last stand to enslave the world.

The amiable Robert "Scully" Sullivan is a long-time A.I.M. member. He's one of our most distinguished mercenaries and perhaps the most respected among his peers. Scully holds the A.I.M. record for the highest number of combat engagements in almost as many different countries. Need someone to snap the last breath from an enemy? Then Sullivan's your man. Scully is well-known for his skills with a bladed weapon.

This former GSG 9 man has only one weakness. Dr. Laura Colin, who he met during a special training in Lübeck. He is an absolute Pro, Night operations are his strength. He can also shoot the nuts out of a squirrel's paws from 3 km. Has the talent to become a trainer.

Len is a leader type, infectious to others. A bit of a joker, with a strange sense of humour. Like Janno Allik he originates from Estonia and has a German mother. Unhappy childhood in Siberia, but made him harder. Night operations and automatic weapons are his greatest strengths.

Magic's calm, cool, and collected demeanor sets the tone for battle. In peak physical condition, he displays razor-sharp reflexes and catlike agility. Magic can ferret out danger with astonishing acumen. And with his lethal marksmanship, he quickly and efficiently puts an end to any threat. He's nicknamed Magic due to the way doors seem to open up in front of him.

This ex-Ranger and sniper has turned stalking into an art form. The Shadow could be lurking about right next to you and chances are you'd never know it. That snow drift, sand dune, or piece of shrubbery could be him. Kyle Simmons has made it his business to blend into any environment for any length of time. The Shadow brings his own camouflage supplies and he wishes to make it known up-front that he doesn't share.

Otherwise known as The Assassin, if you come face-to-face with Carl Sheppards, chances are you're on your last breath. The Reaper takes pride in his patience. He strides silently and uses his stealth skills to gain easy access to his victims. Carl also gets a kick out of looking them in the eye just before they die. One of his former CO's described him as "potentially too dangerous even for A.I.M. service."

Janno, a born Estonian with a German mother. Spent most of his time with the Russian military. Likes to cook for friends and enjoys the good life. But is top fit, a bear of a man, prefers heavy weapons. Locks are no problem and no obstacle.
Prefers to work together with fellow Estonian Vilde, who he met while stationed in East Germany.

Former L.A.P.D. SWAT team sharpshooter, Sgt. Charlene Higgens left the force for A.I.M. so she could work alongside her husband, Ron Higgens. They prefer to take assignments together since operate with the precision of a solitary unit. If you listen to them though, you'd never realize they're married; they rarely acknowledge their personal lives during work. Raven is qualified in night operations.

The born Rumanian came to Germany with her parents, as she was a child just before their cover was blown. Her arrangement against unjust dictators she has from her parents. She studied medicine and came to the mercenary business through her partner Rudolf Steiger. A totally passionate mercenary!

This full Pro is a former member of the GDR Folks army and comes from an old Prussian family. After the reunification of Germany, Henning began an extremely successful career as a merc. Early on he was an adviser in Cuba, now his list of foreign exp. is as long as your arm. Heavy and automatic weapons are in the best hands with Henning.

Following in his uncle's footsteps won't be easy for Igor. His uncle Ivan Dolvich is legendary. Igor may be new to A.I.M. but he is by no means new to armed combat--he developed his abilities for stealth while in action in Chechnya. Most importantly, his bloodline speaks volumes about his potential. Igor and Ivan are already being referred to as the Russian "I-Team."

Lucky comes from Belgium and has not really earned his nickname as he seems to be followed by bad luck. Fatherless childhood, many experiences. Then he discovered someone having a go at his little sister and in his rage, broke the persons neck: an accident. The victim was unfortunately a VIP and therefore Luc had go underground by the mercenaries.

The Russian born Victor Kolesnikov is exactly who would be called a Hard case. Automatic weapons are his area of skill. He very often seeks solitude and does not speak any more than necessary which is why his comrades call him Monk. A fitting nick name for this taciturn pro, who is also a camouflage expert.

Initially trained as an emergency room nurse, Fox Guzzman was recently featured in "Babes and Bullets," a prestigious monthly mercenary magazine. Her article on controlling hemorrhaging while on the battlefield was almost as highly praised as her centerfold layout. Guzzman has recently spent a lot of time on the firing range and it has paid off handsomely.

Due to numerous employee complaints concerning his refusal to follow orders, Fidel "Leave me alone, I'm busy!" Dahan was recently suspended for a thirty-day period. Promising a changed attitude, he was returned to active duty by A.I.M. though because he is so proficient with both firearms and explosives. Fidel hasn't quite been himself lately.
The Insurgent Heavy Class
The Insurgent Medium Class
The Insurgent Light Class
The Security Heavy Class
The Security Medium Class
The Security Light Class

Adding the high velocity StuK 40 7.5cm gun to the StuG III creates a true multirole assault gun. Capable of destroying tanks at great range as well as smashing defenses and enemy concentrations with high-explosive rounds.

descriptions.name: StuG III Ausf. G Assault Gun
descriptions.market_name: StuG III Ausf. G Assault Gun

descriptions.value: The T-34 Medium Tank combines good armor, machine guns, and a versatile 76mm main gun. Wide tracks and a 500hp diesel engine provide mobility and speed.
The most well-rounded heavy tank in the war, the Tiger's mix of armor, mobility, and firepower is legendary. The deadly 88mm KwK 36 gun can crush the armor of any enemy tank.
The BM-13 Katyusha rocket truck carries 16 132mm rockets. Cheap but effective, 'Stalin's Organ' and its fearsome-sounding projectiles have a detrimental effect on enemy morale.
The SdKfz 251 Half-track is a robust and reliable MG-armed vehicle that offers good protection against small-arms fire and provides a stable firing platform for troops inside.
The T-70 Light Tank is mobile and has good armor for a light tank. The 45mm gun resides in a one-man turret; commander has to choose targets, fire guns, and direct the driver.
Designed to counter enemy fighter-bombers, this anti-aircraft support tank uses a rapid-firing 3.7cm gun to frequently engage soft ground targets. A deadly and rare vehicle.
They have heads that are circles, and don't like right angles.
Although hats might fit on their heads better, they don't have families or a soul
If you look closely into their eyes, you'll notice a distinct lack of a soul.
A polygon with three corners, a hero to all, but ultimately alone forever?
Feel the rhythm, feel the rhyme, get on up, it's uhmm.. an enormous raccoon trying to eat you?
Hold on, keep your eyes closed... Do you trust me? I'm flying!!!!!!!!!!
Fly me to the moon!!
Choo Choo! Wait that's a train not a boat. Whatever you get the point.
They live short lives, but the good news is they die alone, cold, and in the dark. YAY!

descriptions.value: It will be more like a funeral than a bachelor party when you run into these handsome Zeds.
It’s lady’s night in Navezgane, which means free murky water and stale beer at Buzz’s bar.
You might need a few Medkits and Breath Mints for this close encounter.
Don’t get pulled over by this guy, he may throw the book and some puke at you.
If you can take down one of these frozen bad boys you might get a fire axe.
Playing doctor could be fun but not with this beauty.
Don’t let this guy hit you or it will be Barbecued Survivor for dinner.
You’ll need a bigger wall to keep this guy out.
Grimm loves who? Creepy crawlies. That's who.

Two "pilots" have fused with a bio-mechanical, two-legged, hairy beast. Both riders have retained their own brains and weapon systems, allowing each to think, act and attack independently of the other. There are three types of missiles aboard: one for rider 1, one for rider 2, and a 3rd, more powerful weapon launched when both pilots pull their triggers simultaneously.

Don’t let the name fool you! There’s nothing motherly in this creature whatsoever. This giant bee has a robo-human head, carries a sickle in her left hand and keeps three "children" dangling from chains.

This extremely large humanoid measures over 4 meters in height and has an insect's face.

This creature is very deadly – it approaches its enemies quietly, in a crouched position, then straightens up and releases energy waves from its massive brain. During combat it gets invisble for some time, which makes it nearly impossible to hit it.
